title: Rendly Real-Time Message Catalog
description: The single source of truth for every frame exchanged over the Rendly real-time WebSocket (chat + 1-on-1 huddle signaling). Mirrors the Sentinel contract dialect: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12, dispatch is SOLELY via oneOf + the unique `msg_type` const on each variant (the consts are unique and the variants are mutually exclusive, so exactly one matches a valid frame). All objects are closed (additionalProperties:false) and all fields are bounded (maxLength/maximum/maxItems): closed schemas remove a silent smuggling channel and bounded fields remove a DoS-via-parse vector. On SERVER->client frames, tenant_id is the SERVER-RESOLVED value from the verified access token (see the contracts/openapi.yaml `oauth2` scheme and contracts/ids.md JWT->ID binding); it lets the client independently confirm the connection's tenant. CLIENT->server frames deliberately OMIT tenant_id (and user_id) -- the server resolves them from the session token and NEVER trusts a client-supplied value. The identity-agnostic `error` frame carries no tenant_id (it can fire before identity resolves), mirroring the REST Error envelope. HONESTY BOUNDARIES (non-removable): (1) huddle signaling is 1-on-1 ONLY -- exactly two peers; group/multi-party huddles are R-011 (post-investment) and are out of scope. (2) The `inspection` object is a SEAM ONLY -- R-001 reserves the result fields and the fail-closed `chat.ack` semantics; the actual PII/injection/secret detection is R-008 and is NOT implemented here. Huddle MEDIA (the P2P WebRTC streams) is NEVER content-inspected; only chat message content and signaling metadata pass the inspection seam. (3) The `archival` object carries hash-chain-ready fields DEFINE-ONLY -- immutable archiving (the actual chain construction/verification) is R-009 and is NOT implemented here.
$comment: Dispatch is normative via oneOf + the per-variant `msg_type` const under Draft 2020-12. There is no discriminator keyword reliance.

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Server-resolved from the access token, never a raw client value. The pattern bounds it to a UUID character shape (no control chars / CRLF) as a log-injection defense, matching client_msg_id/request_id.

user_id.description: Stable user identifier (UUID v4). LOCKED/IMMUTABLE per contracts/ids.md. Opaque surrogate, never PII. UUID-shape pattern bounds it against log injection.

channel_id.description: Channel identifier (UUID v4), tenant-scoped. UUID-shape pattern bounds it against log injection.

message_id.description: Message identifier (UUID v4), server-assigned. Doubles as the archival record id for the message. UUID-shape pattern bounds it against log injection.

huddle_id.description: 1-on-1 huddle session identifier (UUID v4), server-assigned. Doubles as the archival record id for the huddle. UUID-shape pattern bounds it against log injection.

client_msg_id.description: Client-generated idempotency / ack-correlation key. Charset restricted to [A-Za-z0-9._-] to forbid control characters and whitespace (log-injection defense). Echoed back on chat.ack.

request_id.description: Correlation id for an error frame, matching the X-Request-Id correlation convention in contracts/openapi.yaml.

text_content.description: Chat message body. Bounded at 16 KiB to remove a DoS-via-parse vector. Never logged in plaintext; never echoed in error frames.

content_type.description: Rendering hint for the message body.

sha256_hex.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 digest, or null. RESERVED (define-only) for R-009 immutable archiving; R-001 never computes or verifies it.

ArchivalMeta.description: Hash-chain-ready archival metadata carried on every durable record (chat message, huddle). DEFINE-ONLY: R-001 sets record_id/created_at/seq; the hash fields are RESERVED and left null until R-009 builds the tamper-evident chain. Mirrors the Sentinel F-003 audit pattern (sequence + prev-hash + content-hash) without implementing the chain. See ADR-0001 D3.

schema_version.description: Archival envelope version. Bumped only by an ADR + migration.

record_id.description: The durable record id (= message_id for a message, = huddle_id for a huddle).

seq.description: Monotonic per-channel (messages) / per-tenant (huddles) ordering sequence. The ordering field a later hash chain (R-009) links over.

InspectionResult.description: Result of the Sentinel safety inspection seam (FORK D). SEAM ONLY -- R-008 implements detection; R-001 only reserves the shape and the fail-closed semantics. status=pass on any DELIVERED chat.message (fail-closed pre-send means only passed content is delivered). status=blocked or seam_unavailable appears on a chat.ack for a rejected send. `detectors` is reserved for per-detector findings (category + outcome) and carries metadata only -- NEVER the offending content. See ADR-0001 D4.

status.description: pass = inspected and allowed. blocked = a detector blocked the content. seam_unavailable = the inspection seam could not complete -> fail-closed BLOCK (Sentinel non-negotiable #5), never a silent pass.

category.description: Reserved detector category aligned with the Sentinel F-005 detectors.

Signal.description: A single WebRTC signaling payload for a 1-on-1 huddle. Dispatch by the `kind` const. Group/SFU signaling is out of scope (1-on-1 only).

SessionWelcome.description: Server->client. First frame after the WebSocket upgrade authenticates (the access token is presented on the upgrade request; see contracts/openapi.yaml GET /realtime). Confirms the server-resolved identity so the client never asserts its own tenant_id/user_id.

ChatSend.description: Client->server. Send a chat message to a channel. The client supplies only client_msg_id (idempotency), channel_id, and content; tenant_id and sender are server-resolved. The server runs the inspection seam BEFORE delivery/persist (FORK D, synchronous fail-closed) and replies with chat.ack.

ChatMessage.description: Server->client. A delivered chat message (broadcast to channel members). This is the canonical durable message envelope: it carries the archival-ready fields (FORK C, baked-now) and the inspection result (always status=pass, since fail-closed pre-send means only passed content is ever delivered).

inspection.description: On a DELIVERED message the seam status is always pass (fail-closed pre-send means only passed content is delivered). Narrows the shared InspectionResult to status=pass.

ChatAck.description: Server->client. The synchronous result of a chat.send (FORK D). status=accepted -> the message was inspected, passed, persisted, and assigned message_id (delivery follows via chat.message). status=blocked -> the inspection seam blocked it OR the seam could not complete (fail-closed); error_code is set, the message is NOT delivered, and the inspection result (if any) carries metadata only. message_id is present IFF accepted; error_code is present IFF blocked.

error_code.description: Present IFF status=blocked. message_blocked = a detector blocked the content (R-008). inspection_unavailable = the inspection seam could not complete -> fail-closed BLOCK. Never echoes the offending content.

ChatRead.description: Client->server. Read receipt: marks the channel read up to and including up_to_message_id.

TypingSet.description: Client->server. Set this user's typing state in a channel.

TypingUpdate.description: Server->client. Broadcast that a user's typing state changed in a channel.

PresenceSet.description: Client->server. Set this user's presence status.

PresenceUpdate.description: Server->client. Broadcast a user's presence status to subscribers.

HuddleInvite.description: Client->server. Start a 1-on-1 huddle with exactly one peer. The server assigns the huddle_id and signals the peer. 1-ON-1 ONLY: there is no participant list -- group huddles are R-011 (post-investment).

HuddleUpdate.description: Server->client. 1-on-1 huddle lifecycle. The two peers are the inviter and exactly one peer_user_id. archival is present once the huddle reaches a durable (ended) state so R-009 can archive the session record.

SignalSend.description: Client->server. WebRTC signaling for a 1-on-1 huddle, carried over the SAME chat WebSocket (FORK E). The server relays it to the single peer as signal.relay. Signaling metadata is governed by the inspection seam; the resulting MEDIA stream is P2P and NOT content-inspected (honesty boundary).

SignalRelay.description: Server->client. A relayed WebRTC signaling payload from the single huddle peer (FORK E, over the chat WebSocket). from_user_id identifies the peer that sent it.

ErrorFrame.description: Server->client. A real-time protocol/transport error. Like the REST Error envelope, message is a FIXED template chosen SOLELY by error_code -- it never echoes frame content, field names, or PII. Correlate with server-side audit via request_id.

message.description: Fixed template chosen SOLELY by error_code; never echoes frame content, field names, or PII. Enumerated so no request-derived interpolation is possible (mirrors the REST Error.message discipline). The code->message pairing is the implementation's responsibility and is covered by a unit test.
enum: The connection is not authenticated or the token has expired.
The real-time frame is invalid or violates a field constraint.
The message exceeds the maximum allowed size.
Rate limit exceeded. Slow down and retry.
Content was blocked by the safety inspection seam.
The safety inspection seam is unavailable; the send was blocked.
An internal error occurred. The frame was not processed.

examples.message: The real-time frame is invalid or violates a field constraint.
